The Last King of Scotland (2006)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $17,606,684Details
International Box Office $31,548,687
Worldwide Box Office $49,155,371
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $17,609,460
Total $17,609,460
Further financial details...

Synopsis

In an incredible twist of fate, a Scottish doctor on a Ugandan medical mission becomes irreversibly entangled with one of the world’s most barbaric figures: Idi Amin. Impressed by Dr. Garrigan’s brazen attitude in a moment of crisis, the newly self-appointed Ugandan President Amin hand picks him as his personal physician and closest confidante. Though Garrigan is at first flattered and fascinated by his new position, he soon awakens to Amin’s savagery—and his own complicity in it. Horror and betrayal ensue as Garrigan tries to right his wrongs and escape Uganda alive.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$142,899 (0.8% of total gross)
Legs:10.51 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:35.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$6,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/540 max. theaters, 9.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $30,923,980

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2006 (Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: January 12th, 2007 (Wide), released as The Last King Of Scotland (United Kingdom)
Video Release: April 17th, 2007 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for some strong violence and gruesome images, sexual content and language.
Running Time: 121 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue
Geographical setting: Africa, Uganda
Plot point: Biography, Coup d’Etat, Political
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1970s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, DNA Films, FilmFour, UK Film Council, Scottish Screen, Cowboy Films, Slate Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom, Germany
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

In an incredible twist of fate, a Scottish doctor on a Ugandan medical mission becomes irreversibly entangled with one of the world’s most barbaric figures: Idi Amin. Impressed by Dr. Garrigan’s brazen attitude in a moment of crisis, the newly self-appointed Ugandan President Amin hand picks him as his personal physician and closest confidante. Though Garrigan is at first flattered and fascinated by his new position, he soon awakens to Amin’s savagery—and his own complicity in it. Horror and betrayal ensue as Garrigan tries to right his wrongs and escape Uganda alive.

Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $30,923,980

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2006 (Wide) by Fox Searchlight
International Releases: January 12th, 2007 (Wide), released as The Last King Of Scotland (United Kingdom)
Video Release: April 17th, 2007 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for some strong violence and gruesome images, sexual content and language.
Running Time: 121 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Epilogue
Geographical setting: Africa, Uganda
Plot point: Biography, Coup d’Etat, Political
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Time period setting: 1970s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Fox Searchlight Pictures, DNA Films, FilmFour, UK Film Council, Scottish Screen, Cowboy Films, Slate Films
Production Countries: United Kingdom, Germany
Languages: English
